name: Preston Love
abstract: Preston Haynes Love (April 26, 1921 – February 12, 2004) was an American saxophonist, bandleader, and songwriter from Omaha, Nebraska, United States, best known as a sideman for jazz and rhythm and blues artists like Count Basie and Ray Charles.

concerts.label: Kool Jazz Festival: The Johnny Otis Show

about_quotes.speaker: Jo Jones
about_quotes.evidence: I did 'The King' with Basie and Preston Love, who had replaced Earl Warren during the time - he and I both went to the Pennsylvania Station... Earl sat back in the band. I said, 'You got it, Shad, I'll be back later.'

about_quotes.speaker: Ernie Fields
about_quotes.evidence: The transcript identifies Preston Love as the interviewer ('Conducted by Preston Love') and the interviewee addresses him as 'Preston' during the conversation, but no specific professional or personal relationship history between Ernie Fields and Preston Love is described in the text.

about_quotes.speaker: Gus Johnson
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] And Preston Love was in the band. [INTERVIEWEE] Preston Love.

about_quotes.speaker: Freddie Green
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Now you've got Preston Love and George Dorsey on altos. ... [INTERVIEWER] Preston Love, George Dorsey, Buddy Tate, and --

about_quotes.speaker: Buddy Tate
about_quotes.evidence: with Preston Love playing first and -- beautiful sounding band.
